vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1847: delete the dead projection_cache table + write-through paths (unused after PAN-1920)

plan.title: Delete the dead projection_cache table + write-through paths (unused after PAN-1920)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: After PAN-1920 the entire projection_cache table is dead: ProjectionCache.load() has zero callers (reconstruct-cache.ts never reads it), yet two write-through save() paths still run on every state change — the read-model 'dashboard' snapshot (historically a CPU hog) and the agent-state-service 'agent-runtime:<id>' rows. The original 'reclaim 1.4 GB' premise was empirically disproven: the 12,125 agent-runtime: rows are 1.93 MB (0.14% of the 1379 MB DB); the real ~1 GB is the events table (agent.output_received = 721 MB), tracked separately as PAN-1925.
narratives.Proposal: Per the no-bandaids/root-cause rule, DELETE the dead table rather than build a GC for a cache nobody reads. Remove both write-through save() paths, delete the projection-cache.ts service module and its imports, stop creating the table (base schema + Bun path + bundled cost hook) and DROP it on existing DBs via a forward migration, then lock the deletion with a regression test. This is zero behavioral change — getSnapshot builds snapshots live via buildSnapshot() and never depended on load().
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT touch the events table or agent.output_received retention (the real ~1 GB; separate issue PAN-1925).
- Do NOT attempt to reclaim 1.4 GB or 'shrink the DB substantially' — that premise is retired; ~7.5 MB is freed and reclaimed automatically by the existing incremental_vacuum.
- Do NOT add a VACUUM or VACUUM INTO step (auto_vacuum is already INCREMENTAL with a 15-min reclaimer).
- Do NOT build a garbage collector / pruner for projection_cache (rejected — deletion supersedes it).
- Do NOT change runtime behavior: keep the agentRuntimeById reducer fold, the runtime mirror, agent.state_restored, and PAN-1920 reconstruction.
- Do NOT weaken or edit tests/unit/lib/reconstruct/no-circular-source.test.ts (re-introduction guard).
- Do NOT edit historical archives under docs/prds/** or vbrief/completed/** that mention projection_cache.
- Do NOT hand-edit the bundled artifact sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js — regenerate it from source.

autoDecisions.summary: Schema removal removes BOTH the base CREATE and the historical 'if (currentVersion < 5)' create block, adds an 'if (currentVersion < 56) DROP TABLE IF EXISTS projection_cache' migration, and bumps SCHEMA_VERSION 55->56.
autoDecisions.rationale: Removing the base CREATE stops recreation on every DB open; the DROP migration cleans existing DBs once; removing the historical block keeps the regenerated cost hook grep-clean. Verified safe: no migration test migrates a DB from version < 5 or expects projection_cache (schema-migrations.test.ts).

auto_vacuum is already INCREMENTAL and src/lib/database/index.ts:108 reclaims the freelist every 15 minutes; the ~7.5 MB freed by DROP is reclaimed automatically. Disk reclaim is not a goal of this issue.

autoDecisions.summary: The bundled cost hook is regenerated from source via 'npm run build:scripts', never hand-edited.
autoDecisions.rationale: record-cost-event.js is a tsdown bundle that transitively includes schema.ts; editing the source + rebuilding is the only correct way to drop the embedded CREATE TABLE without divergence.

items.title: Remove the read-model 'dashboard'-key projection_cache write-through

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/read-model.ts remove the projectionCache declaration (~375), all four `projectionCache?.save(buildSnapshot());` calls (~428, ~463, ~611, ~663), the `initProjectionCache` dynamic import + init assignment (~496-499), and fix the bootstrap comment (~491-492) that claims 'projection_cache write-through continues below'. Preserve every surrounding behavior: the prune+log+state update around 460-465, the checkpoint-reconciliation log around 609-612, and the onIssuesChanged state update around 659-663. buildSnapshot() must remain defined and returned by getSnapshot (~467).

items.title: After the change, `grep -n projectionCache src/dashboard/server/read-model.ts` returns no matches (declaration, all four save() calls, the import, and the init are removed).
getSnapshot still returns buildSnapshot() and the prune/log/state-update logic that surrounded the former save() sites is preserved (verifiable from the diff).
`npm run typecheck` produces no unused-variable or unresolved-import error for read-model.ts.
Remove the agent-state-service 'agent-runtime:' projection_cache write-through

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/services/agent-state-service.ts remove the DB write only, exactly per the before/after in .pan/drafts/PAN-1847.md: drop `getSharedDb` from the event-store import (~33), the AGENT_RUNTIME_KEY_PREFIX const (~40), the `const db = getSharedDb(); const upsertStmt = ...` block (~107-121), the `UpsertStmt` type alias (~167), the upsertStmt parameter of applyEventToRef and its `for...upsertStmt.run(...)` loop (~189-217), and update the stale comments at ~20 and ~107-112. KEEP applyReducerEvent (the agentRuntimeById fold), setAgentRuntimeMirror(next), and the SubscriptionRef.update return — these drive live UI runtime snapshots. Update the store.subscribe wiring (~149) to call applyEventToRef(ref, ev) with no upsertStmt arg.

items.title: `grep -nE 'projection_cache|upsertStmt|AGENT_RUNTIME_KEY_PREFIX|getSharedDb|UpsertStmt' src/dashboard/server/services/agent-state-service.ts` returns no matches.

applyEventToRef still calls applyReducerEvent and Effect.runSync(setAgentRuntimeMirror(next)) and returns next — the reducer fold and runtime mirror update are preserved (verifiable from the diff against the PRD after-snippet).
tests/contracts/agent-runtime-reducers.test.ts still passes unmodified: applying agent.activity_changed through the reducer produces the same agentRuntimeById entry as before.
Remove projection_cache wiring from the event store

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/event-store.ts remove the Bun-path `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projection_cache (...)` block (~156-163) and the post-init dynamic `import('./services/projection-cache.js').then(({ initProjectionCache }) => initProjectionCache(db))` block (~392-395). Leave the surrounding index creation and store init intact.

items.title: `grep -n projection_cache src/dashboard/server/event-store.ts` returns no matches and the file no longer imports services/projection-cache.
openEventDb still creates the events table + indexes and initEventStore still returns the store (no other init step removed).
`npm run typecheck` passes for event-store.ts.
Delete the projection-cache.ts service module and its dedicated unit test

narrative.Action: Delete src/dashboard/server/services/projection-cache.ts entirely (createProjectionCache, initProjectionCache, getProjectionCache, load, save, the ProjectionCache interface, CACHE_KEY, FLUSH_DEBOUNCE_MS). Delete tests/dashboard/projection-cache.test.ts (it imports createProjectionCache and tests the deleted service). This bead is safe only after B1, B2, B3 have removed every importer.

items.title: `ls src/dashboard/server/services/projection-cache.ts` exits non-zero — the service module is deleted.
`ls tests/dashboard/projection-cache.test.ts` exits non-zero — the dedicated test is deleted.
`grep -rn "services/projection-cache" src/ tests/` returns no import; `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` report no unresolved-module error.
Stop creating projection_cache in the schema; DROP it via a forward migration; regenerate the cost hook

narrative.Action: In src/lib/database/schema.ts: remove the base-schema `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projection_cache (...)` block (~486-492) and the historical `if (currentVersion < 5) { ... CREATE TABLE ... projection_cache ... }` block (~817-827); add `if (currentVersion < 56) { db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS projection_cache'); }` immediately before `db.pragma(\`user_version = ${SCHEMA_VERSION}\`)`; change `SCHEMA_VERSION = 55` to `56` (~24). Then run `npm run build:scripts` to regenerate sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js (tsdown bundles schema.ts) and commit the regenerated artifact.

items.title: SCHEMA_VERSION equals 56; schema.ts contains no `CREATE TABLE ... projection_cache`; a `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS projection_cache` runs under `if (currentVersion < 56)`.
A fresh DB created through runMigrations has no projection_cache table: `SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='projection_cache'` returns no row.
Running runMigrations on a DB seeded at user_version 54 produces user_version 56 and contains no projection_cache table; tests/unit/lib/database/schema-migrations.test.ts still passes unmodified.
After `npm run build:scripts`, `grep -c projection_cache sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js` returns 0, and the regenerated artifact is committed.
Remove the orphaned projection-cache mock from the PAN-1920 boot test

narrative.Action: In tests/unit/dashboard/server/reconstruct-boot.test.ts remove the `vi.mock('.../services/projection-cache.js', ...)` block (~48-51) and the now-unused dbPrepareSpy / getSharedDbMock.mockReturnValue({ prepare: dbPrepareSpy }) scaffolding (~31, ~71) that only existed to satisfy the deleted agent-state-service upsert. No assertion references the upsert, so no assertion is removed. The two PAN-1920 bootstrap tests must stay green.

items.title: reconstruct-boot.test.ts contains no `vi.mock` of services/projection-cache.js and no dbPrepareSpy that was only feeding the removed projection_cache upsert.

`npm test` produces a passing result for both PAN-1920 bootstrap tests: the AgentStateService test confirms reconstructCache runs, readFrom does not, and subscribe receives the forward wiring; the ReadModelService test seeds from reconstructCache.
Add a regression test that locks the projection_cache deletion

narrative.Action: Add a new test (e.g. tests/unit/dashboard/server/projection-cache-removed.test.ts) with two assertions: (1) a fresh DB built via runMigrations has no projection_cache table (sqlite_master query empty); (2) a source guard reading src/dashboard/server/{read-model.ts,event-store.ts,services/agent-state-service.ts} finds no `initProjectionCache`, no `.save(buildSnapshot`, and no `services/projection-cache` import. This locks both halves of the deletion against re-introduction.

items.title: The new test asserts a fresh DB built via runMigrations has no projection_cache table, and the assertion passes.
The new test's source guard verifies no dashboard server file references initProjectionCache, .save(buildSnapshot, or services/projection-cache, and the assertion passes.
The new test runs and passes under `npm test`.
Update stale comments that describe projection_cache as a live path

metadata.acJustification: Comment-accuracy bead made false by the deletion; two mechanical grep/gate ACs suffice.

narrative.Action: Update the three comments made factually false by the deletion so they no longer describe projection_cache as a live persistence path: src/lib/agent-runtime.ts:6 ('SubscriptionRef + projection_cache'), src/lib/agents.ts:2281 ('SubscriptionRef -> projection_cache rows keyed agent-runtime:<id>'), packages/contracts/src/events.ts:363 ('snapshot from projection_cache. Not emitted by hooks.'). Keep edits minimal and comment-only; do not change code.

items.title: `grep -n projection_cache src/lib/agent-runtime.ts src/lib/agents.ts packages/contracts/src/events.ts` returns no matches (the stale live-path references are removed).
After the comment-only edits, `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` each exits 0 (no code changed).
